title: Lepechinia conferta
intro: Lepechinia conferta, also known as Mexican hyssop or Mexican lepechini, is a perennial shrubby herb native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Lepechinia conferta's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and gastrointestinal disorders.
